title: elymus repens subsp. pseudocaesius
intro: Elymus repens subsp. pseudocaesius, also known as the couch grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Elymus repens. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Elymus repens subsp. pseudocaesius include its ability to help with digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or infusement for its medicinal use, although modern herbal preparations may also include the plant in capsule or tincture form.
